lines: +    // Mistral Instruct v0.3 - uses [INST]/[/INST] markers without [SYSTEM_PROMPT]

+        LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v3\n");

content.text: Now let me verify both changes look correct:

Both changes are in place and correct. Here's a summary of what was added to `common/chat.cpp`:

---

### 1. New handler function (lines 1634–1666)

`common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3` is inserted just before `common_chat_try_specialized_template()`. It:

- Collects any `system` message into `sys`
- On the **first `user` turn**: prepends `<s>[INST] {sys}\n{content} [/INST]` (or `<s>[INST] {content} [/INST]` when there's no system message)
- On **subsequent `user` turns**: appends `[INST] {content} [/INST]`
- On **`assistant` turns**: appends ` {content}</s>`
- Sets `data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY` and returns

This produces the exact multi-turn format from the spec:
```
<s>[INST] {system}\n{user1} [/INST] {assistant1}</s>[INST] {user2} [/INST]
```

### 2. Detection logic (lines 1724–1731)

Added inside `common_chat_try_specialized_template()` before `return std::nullopt`:

```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

The check for `[INST]` + `[/INST]` **and absence of** `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` correctly distinguishes Mistral Instruct v0.3 from the existing Ministral 3 handler (which requires `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`). No existing handlers were modified.

result:
Both changes are in place and correct. Here's a summary of what was added to `common/chat.cpp`:

---

### 1. New handler function (lines 1634–1666)

`common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3` is inserted just before `common_chat_try_specialized_template()`. It:

- Collects any `system` message into `sys`
- On the **first `user` turn**: prepends `<s>[INST] {sys}\n{content} [/INST]` (or `<s>[INST] {content} [/INST]` when there's no system message)
- On **subsequent `user` turns**: appends `[INST] {content} [/INST]`
- On **`assistant` turns**: appends ` {content}</s>`
- Sets `data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY` and returns

This produces the exact multi-turn format from the spec:
```
<s>[INST] {system}\n{user1} [/INST] {assistant1}</s>[INST] {user2} [/INST]
```

### 2. Detection logic (lines 1724–1731)

Added inside `common_chat_try_specialized_template()` before `return std::nullopt`:

```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

The check for `[INST]` + `[/INST]` **and absence of** `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` correctly distinguishes Mistral Instruct v0.3 from the existing Ministral 3 handler (which requires `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`). No existing handlers were modified.
